ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #4, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Dec-19-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Geoffrey Adams for and with Antonio Rivera (Co-Trustee) and for Gloria Rivera (Co-Trustee) not present.

Mr. Adams stated that when the car was purchased they did not petition the Court for approval, that the car was not listed in the third accounting as an asset, that they left it titled in their names, and that it was not a liability to the trust. The Court inquired as to the policy limit. Mr. Rivera stated the policy limit was $500,000.00, that the car was in his wife's name, and that Sandy (his daughter) does not drive because she is handicapped. Mr. Adams stated that they would make an assignment and file it in a couple of weeks. Mr. Rivera stated he had some concerns regarding the trust, and briefly explained to the Court the basis for the litigation. Mr. Adams noted that Mr. Rivera would file a petition to address his concerns. The matter was taken under submission and would be granted (upon review of the written order).

Judge: Raymond Arata. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: J. Alameda./R. Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #3, REPORT
Off Calendar Sep-06-2006 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Geoffrey Adams appearing for and with special needs trustees Antonio Rivera and Gloria Rivera. Van issue and notice issue addressed. The trustees need help with using the wheelchair on trips. Court suggests them to check with the health plan for a motorized wheelchair. All big purchases will need prior court approval. Matter granted. Order signed. Bond is reduced to $100,000.00. Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Susan Lee. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Mizota.
